title: tephrosia dasyphylla
intro: Tephrosia dasyphylla, also known as Kallu or Horsefly Tree, is a flowering plant species native to Africa and parts of Asia, known for its unique ability to repel horseflies and other insects. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits are said to include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Tephrosia dasyphylla can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
